heading: Overview
body:
A MongoDB Search analyzer prepares a set of documents to be indexed by
performing a series of operations to transform, filter, and group
sequences of characters. You can define a custom analyzer to suit your
specific indexing needs from the Atlas UI.

A custom MongoDB Search analyzer is made up of a [tokenizers](tokenizers-ref), an optional list of
[character filter](character-filters), and an optional list of [token filters](token-filters-ref).
When MongoDB Search analyzes text with a custom analyzer, the text passes through the character filters first, then the tokenizer,
and then the token filters.
